step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("Optimal anti-rotational design in surgical blades")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on my review of the gathered information regarding surgical practices and specifically focusing on the context surrounding anti-rotational mechanisms in surgical instrumentation, I found evidence suggesting that the ideal offset for minimizing unwanted rotations and achieving stable fixation varies widely among different procedures. However, given the emphasis on precision and minimal trauma particularly noted in orthopedics and reconstructive surgery (as seen in reverse shoulder arthroplasty and radial keratotomy correction discussions), values closer to what we initially considered seem supported. Most references did not specify exact numerical thresholds but generally leaned towards smaller adjustments rather than large ones. Therefore, considering our initial stance favoring tight controls to mitigate adverse effects yet still needing sufficient functionality for effective treatment, options above ~2mm appear less favored despite some variability observed across disciplines.
